name: Catfish Pecan Meuniere
recipeIngredient: 4 catfish or trout fillets, (6 to 6 1/2 oz each)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a shallow bowl, combine 1 cup of flour with 2 teaspoons of Rustic Rub.
2. In another shallow bowl, blend 2 eggs and 1/2 cup of milk together.
3. Season the fish fillets with the remaining 2 teaspoons of Rustic Rub.
4. Heat 1/2 cup of vegetable oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat.
5. Dredge each fillet evenly in the flour mixture, then dip in the egg mixture, and coat again in the flour.
6. When the oil is hot but not smoking, place the fillets in the skillet. Cook for 3 to 4 minutes on each side, or until golden brown.
7. Transfer the fillets to a warm platter. Remove all oil from the skillet and wipe clean with paper towels.
8. Return the skillet to the stove. Melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium-high heat.
9. Once the butter foams, add the pecan pieces and stir for about 1 1/2 minutes, or until lightly toasted.
10. Add chopped parsley, minced garlic, lemon juice, Worcestershire sauce, and heavy cream to the skillet. Whisk for 15 seconds and turn off the heat.
11. Stir in 1 teaspoon of salt, 1/4 teaspoon of cayenne, and the remaining butter chips until completely melted.
12. Pour the sauce over the fillets and serve.
